seoTitle: Legerwood vs Leith — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Legerwood and Leith. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Legerwood and Leith.
heading: Legerwood vs Leith — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Legerwood, Tasmania and Leith, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Legerwood has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Leith (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Legerwood or Leith safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Legerwood has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Leith scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Legerwood scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Leith scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Legerwood or Leith?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Legerwood has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
